exercises.title: Configuration Class System
exercises.description: Implement the Flask config class pattern using pure Python. Create a `Config` base class and `DevelopmentConfig`, `ProductionConfig`, `TestingConfig` subclasses. Each should have `DEBUG`, `SECRET_KEY`, `DATABASE_URL`, and `LOG_LEVEL` attributes. Write a `create_app_config(env)` factory function that returns the right config. Print all settings for each environment.
exercises.starterCode:
class Config:
    DEBUG = False
    SECRET_KEY = "change-me-in-production"
    DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///app.db"
    LOG_LEVEL = "WARNING"
    MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH = 16 * 1024 * 1024  # 16 MB

class DevelopmentConfig(Config):
    # TODO: Override: DEBUG=True, LOG_LEVEL='DEBUG',
    # DATABASE_URL='sqlite:///dev.db'
    pass

class ProductionConfig(Config):
    # TODO: Override: DEBUG=False, LOG_LEVEL='ERROR',
    # DATABASE_URL='postgresql://user:pass@prod-db/app',
    # SECRET_KEY='prod-secret-from-env'
    pass

class TestingConfig(Config):
    # TODO: Override: DEBUG=True, TESTING=True, LOG_LEVEL='DEBUG',
    # DATABASE_URL='sqlite:///:memory:'
    # Add TESTING = True
    pass

config_map = {
    "development": DevelopmentConfig,
    "production": ProductionConfig,
    "testing": TestingConfig,
}

def create_app_config(env):
    """Return the config class for the given environment name."""
    # TODO: Return the config class from config_map, default to DevelopmentConfig
    pass

# Print settings for each environment
for env_name in ["development", "production", "testing"]:
    cfg = create_app_config(env_name)
    print(f"--- {env_name.upper()} ---")
    print(f"DEBUG: {cfg.DEBUG}")
    print(f"LOG_LEVEL: {cfg.LOG_LEVEL}")
    print(f"DATABASE_URL: {cfg.DATABASE_URL}")
    print()
exercises.expectedOutput:
--- DEVELOPMENT ---
DEBUG: True
LOG_LEVEL: DEBUG
DATABASE_URL: sqlite:///dev.db

--- PRODUCTION ---
DEBUG: False
LOG_LEVEL: ERROR
DATABASE_URL: postgresql://user:pass@prod-db/app

--- TESTING ---
DEBUG: True
LOG_LEVEL: DEBUG
DATABASE_URL: sqlite:///:memory:
hints: Subclasses override class-level attributes — just define them in the subclass body.
config_map.get(env, DevelopmentConfig) handles unknown environment names gracefully.
Return the class itself (not an instance): return config_map.get(env, DevelopmentConfig)
Access attributes directly on the class: cfg.DEBUG (no need to instantiate).

exercises.title: Environment Variable Processor
exercises.description: Simulate reading configuration from environment variables (provided as a dict). Write `load_config(env_vars)` that reads a dict of env var strings and returns a typed config dict. Handle type conversion: DATABASE_URL (string), DEBUG (bool: 'true'/'1'/'yes'), PORT (int), WORKERS (int), ALLOWED_HOSTS (comma-separated list). Print the loaded config and validate required vars.
exercises.starterCode:
def load_config(env_vars):
    """
    Parse environment variables into a typed config dict.
    Raises ValueError if required vars are missing.
    """
    REQUIRED = ["SECRET_KEY", "DATABASE_URL"]
    # TODO: Check all REQUIRED vars exist in env_vars, raise ValueError if missing

    # TODO: Parse each variable with the correct type:
    # SECRET_KEY: str (required)
    # DATABASE_URL: str (required)
    # DEBUG: bool ('true', '1', 'yes' -> True; anything else -> False)
    # PORT: int (default 8000 if not present)
    # WORKERS: int (default 4 if not present)
    # ALLOWED_HOSTS: list from comma-separated string (default ['localhost'])

    # TODO: Return the config dict
    pass

# Test 1: Complete valid config
env = {
    "SECRET_KEY": "super-secret-key",
    "DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://localhost/mydb",
    "DEBUG": "false",
    "PORT": "5000",
    "WORKERS": "8",
    "ALLOWED_HOSTS": "myapp.com,www.myapp.com,api.myapp.com",
}
config = load_config(env)
print(f"DEBUG type: {type(config['DEBUG']).__name__}, value: {config['DEBUG']}")
print(f"PORT type: {type(config['PORT']).__name__}, value: {config['PORT']}")
print(f"WORKERS type: {type(config['WORKERS']).__name__}, value: {config['WORKERS']}")
print(f"ALLOWED_HOSTS: {config['ALLOWED_HOSTS']}")

# Test 2: Minimal config with defaults
env_minimal = {"SECRET_KEY": "key123", "DATABASE_URL": "sqlite:///app.db"}
config_min = load_config(env_minimal)
print(f"Default PORT: {config_min['PORT']}")
print(f"Default WORKERS: {config_min['WORKERS']}")
print(f"Default HOSTS: {config_min['ALLOWED_HOSTS']}")

# Test 3: Missing required var
try:
    load_config({"SECRET_KEY": "key"})
except ValueError as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
exercises.expectedOutput:
DEBUG type: bool, value: False
PORT type: int, value: 5000
WORKERS type: int, value: 8
ALLOWED_HOSTS: ['myapp.com', 'www.myapp.com', 'api.myapp.com']
Default PORT: 8000
Default WORKERS: 4
Default HOSTS: ['localhost']
Error: Missing required environment variable: DATABASE_URL
hints: For bool: debug_str = env_vars.get('DEBUG', 'false').lower(); debug = debug_str in ('true', '1', 'yes')
For required check: for var in REQUIRED: if var not in env_vars: raise ValueError(f'Missing required environment variable: {var}')

exercises.title: Production Readiness Checker
exercises.description: Write a `check_production_readiness(config)` function that validates a config dict against a production checklist. It should check 10 conditions and print a report showing PASS/FAIL for each. Return the count of failures. Test with a good config and a bad one.
exercises.starterCode:
def check_production_readiness(config):
    """
    Run production readiness checks on a config dict.
    Prints PASS/FAIL for each check and returns failure count.
    """
    checks = [
        # (check_name, condition_that_should_be_True, description)
        ("SECRET_KEY set",       lambda c: bool(c.get("SECRET_KEY")) and c["SECRET_KEY"] != "change-me",
         "Secret key must be set and non-default"),
        ("DEBUG disabled",       lambda c: c.get("DEBUG") == False,
         "Debug must be False in production"),
        ("Database configured",  lambda c: bool(c.get("DATABASE_URL")),
         "Database URL must be configured"),
        ("Port valid",           lambda c: 1 <= c.get("PORT", 0) <= 65535,
         "Port must be 1-65535"),
        ("Workers >= 2",         lambda c: c.get("WORKERS", 0) >= 2,
         "At least 2 workers for production"),
        ("LOG_LEVEL production", lambda c: c.get("LOG_LEVEL", "") in ("WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"),
         "Log level should not be DEBUG in production"),
    ]

    print("=== Production Readiness Check ===")
    failures = 0
    for name, condition, desc in checks:
        # TODO: Run each condition with the config
        # TODO: Print "  [PASS] name" or "  [FAIL] name: desc"
        # TODO: Increment failures if condition returns False
        pass

    print(f"\nResult: {len(checks) - failures}/{len(checks)} checks passed")
    return failures

# Good config
good_config = {
    "SECRET_KEY": "abc123xyz-production-key",
    "DEBUG": False,
    "DATABASE_URL": "postgresql://localhost/prod",
    "PORT": 8000,
    "WORKERS": 4,
    "LOG_LEVEL": "ERROR",
}
print("\n--- Good Config ---")
failures = check_production_readiness(good_config)

# Bad config
bad_config = {
    "SECRET_KEY": "change-me",
    "DEBUG": True,
    "DATABASE_URL": "",
    "PORT": 99999,
    "WORKERS": 1,
    "LOG_LEVEL": "DEBUG",
}
print("\n--- Bad Config ---")
failures = check_production_readiness(bad_config)
exercises.expectedOutput:
--- Good Config ---
=== Production Readiness Check ===
  [PASS] SECRET_KEY set
  [PASS] DEBUG disabled
  [PASS] Database configured
  [PASS] Port valid
  [PASS] Workers >= 2
  [PASS] LOG_LEVEL production

Result: 6/6 checks passed

--- Bad Config ---
=== Production Readiness Check ===
  [FAIL] SECRET_KEY set: Secret key must be set and non-default
  [FAIL] DEBUG disabled: Debug must be False in production
  [FAIL] Database configured: Database URL must be configured
  [FAIL] Port valid: Port must be 1-65535
  [FAIL] Workers >= 2: At least 2 workers for production
  [FAIL] LOG_LEVEL production: Log level should not be DEBUG in production

Result: 0/6 checks passed
hints: If passed: print(f'  [PASS] {name}') else: print(f'  [FAIL] {name}: {desc}'); failures += 1
The condition is a lambda — call it with config: passed = condition(config)
Return failures at the end of the function.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Class Attribute Inheritance
warmup.starterCode:
class Base:
    DEBUG = False
    LEVEL = "WARNING"

class Dev(Base):
    DEBUG = True
    LEVEL = "DEBUG"

class Prod(Base):
    LEVEL = "ERROR"

print(Dev.DEBUG, Dev.LEVEL)
print(Prod.DEBUG, Prod.LEVEL)
print(Base.DEBUG, Base.LEVEL)
warmup.expectedOutput:
True DEBUG
False ERROR
False WARNING
